title: xyris baldwiniana
intro: Xyris baldwiniana is a species of flowering plant in the family Xyridaceae, commonly known as the yellow-eyed grass. It is native to the southeastern United States and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and treat respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of Xyris baldwiniana, such as infusions and tinctures, can be used topically or internally to reap its health benefits, including relieving congestion, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing.
